title: price_volume_scatter.py output
description: Always a flat object — price_volume_scatter.py requires --component. component_breakdown is one row per supplier (the primary axis, since --component is already fixed by the required filter); criticality_breakdown is the same, further split by component_criticality. volume_pct/price_percentile in each array are computed relative to that array's own population.

component_breakdown.description: One row per supplier in the filtered scope. This is what the scatter chart and console table visualize.

avg_price.description: Spend-weighted: total_spend / total_qty, not a plain mean of unit_cost_usd — so PO-size variance doesn't distort price_category.

volume_pct.description: This row's % share of total qty within its own array's population.

price_percentile.description: 0-1. This row's avg_price rank among rows in the same array.

criticality_breakdown.description: One row per (supplier_name, component_criticality) combination present in scope.

avg_price.description: Spend-weighted: total_spend / total_qty, not a plain mean of unit_cost_usd.

volume_pct.description: This row's % share of total qty within criticality_breakdown's population (not the same denominator as component_breakdown's volume_pct).

price_percentile.description: 0-1. This row's avg_price rank among criticality_breakdown rows (not the same population as component_breakdown's price_percentile).
